claim: The myocardial lineage develops from cardiac progenitors of mesodermal origin.

result.claim: The myocardial lineage develops from cardiac progenitors of mesodermal origin.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper 4427392, "Human cardiovascular progenitor cells develop from a KDR+ embryonic-stem-cell-derived population." Proposition 1 from this paper states, "Cardiomyocytes... are distinct mesoderm-derived lineages comprising the functional heart." Furthermore, the abstract of paper 4427392 (Chunk 1) explicitly mentions that these lineages "develop from a common Flk-1+ cardiovascular progenitor that represents one of the earliest stages in mesoderm specification to the cardiovascular lineages." This directly confirms that the myocardial lineage (cardiomyocytes) originates from mesodermal cardiac progenitors. The paper also details the differentiation of KDRlow/C-KITneg cells into contracting cardiomyocytes, further supporting the role of progenitor cells in myocardial development. While the paper's author and year are unknown, the information presented is clear and directly relevant to the claim.

evidence.text: KDRlow/C-KITneg cells differentiate to generate populations consisting of greater than 50% contracting cardiomyocytes when plated in monolayer cultures.
evidence.source: Human cardiovascular progenitor cells develop from a KDR+ embryonic-stem-cell-derived population

evidence.text: Cardiomyocytes, endothelial cells, and vascular smooth muscle cells are distinct mesoderm-derived lineages comprising the functional heart.
evidence.source: Human cardiovascular progenitor cells develop from a KDR+ embryonic-stem-cell-derived population

evidence.text: Studies in the mouse embryo and the mouse embryonic stem cell differentiation model indicate that these three lineages develop from a common Flk-1+ cardiovascular progenitor.
evidence.source: Human cardiovascular progenitor cells develop from a KDR+ embryonic-stem-cell-derived population

evidence.text: Human embryonic-stem-cell-derived embryoid bodies generate a KDRlow/C-KIT(CD117)neg population after induction with activin A, bone morphogenetic protein 4 (BMP4), basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF, also known as FGF2), vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF, also known as VEGFA) and dickkopf homolog 1 (DKK1) in serum-free media.
evidence.source: Human cardiovascular progenitor cells develop from a KDR+ embryonic-stem-cell-derived population

evidence.text: KDRlow/C-KITneg cells differentiate to generate populations consisting of greater than 50% contracting cardiomyocytes when plated in monolayer cultures.
evidence.source: Human cardiovascular progenitor cells develop from a KDR+ embryonic-stem-cell-derived population
